lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Welcome to the Burthorpe Games Rooms!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I play board games?

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: You can challenge someone to a game anywhere in the games rooms by using the right click option. Choose the type of game and then choose the options you want such as time per move. If you want to play a particular

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: game there are challenge rooms dedicated to that game. In the challenge rooms you can see other players ranks by right clicking them, their skill will be displayed instead of their combat level. Once you have enough

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: experience you will be able to use the challenge rooms on the first floor! If your opponent accepts the challenge you will be taken to one of the tables in the main room where you will play your game of choice.

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Once you have finished your game you will go back to the challenge room where you can challenge again!

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Currently we offer Draughts, Runelink, Runesquares, and Runeversi.

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Draughts uses standard rules, apart from: a draw is declared if no piece has been taken or promoted for forty moves. To play Draughts go to the challenge room in the SW corner.

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Yup, you have to get four runes in row. The challenge room for Runelink is in the SE corner.

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Yes, you take it in turns to add a line with the goal of making squares. Everytime you make a square you take another turn. The challenge room for Runesquares is in the SW corner.

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Yep, the aim is to have more of your runes on the board than your opponent. You can take your opponents pieces by trapping them between your own. The challenge room for Runeversi is in the SE corner.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I buy a drink please?

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Certainly miss, our speciality is Asgarnian Ale, we also serve Wizard's Mind Bomb and Dwarven Stout.
